source_pr.title: plan, parser: fix the issue of checking LIMIT and ORDER BY in the UNION statement
source_pr.body:
check union's `select limit / order by` follow [mysql](https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.7/en/union.html)'s logic.

- last `limit`/`order by` affect to whole `union` statement
- to apply `limit`/`order by` to an individual `select`, must place the clause  in braces

## What have you changed? (mandatory)

- add ` IsInBraces` to `SelectStmt`
- modify parser to set `IsInBraces`  to `true` when in `select` place in braces within `union`
- modify `preprocessor.checkUnionSelectList` to check select stmt except last one must in braces or without `limit`/`order by`

## What are the type of the changes (mandatory)?

- Bug fix

## How has this PR been tested (mandatory)?

- unit tests: in executor_test.go
- manual test

## Does this PR affect documentation (docs/docs-cn) update? (optional)

pending...

## Add a few positive/negative examples (optional)

expect:

```
mysql> (select 1 from t limit 1) union (select 2 from t limit 1) limit 2;
Empty set (0.00 sec)
```
but got:

```
mysql> (select 1 from t limit 1) union (select 2 from t limit 1) limit 2;
ERROR 1221 (HY000): Incorrect usage of UNION and LIMIT
```

symptoms: _, err = tk.Exec("(select a from t order by a) union all select a from t limit 1 union all select a from t limit 1") (executor/executor_test.go)
failure_signature: _, err = tk.Exec("(select a from t order by a) union all select a from t limit 1 union all select a from t limit 1") (executor/executor_test.go)
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: _, err = tk.Exec("(select a from t order by a) union all select a from t limit 1 union all select a from t limit 1") (executor/executor_test.go)
